REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY STATES CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE AGAINST DEFENDANT WILLIAM STUART, AND FOR QUIET TITLE AGAINST DEFENDANT MILESTONE FINANCIAL LLC. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT STUART PERSONALLY PARTICIPATED IN OR RATIFIED DEFENDANT MILESTONE'S CONDUCT. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES PLAINTIFF'S TITLE AND THE BASIS FOR HER TITLE; DEFENDANTS CITE NO AUTHORITY FOR THE IMPLICIT PROPOSITION THAT AN INSTRUMENT WHICH THE CHALLENGED PLEADING EXPRESSLY ALLEGES TO BE VOID IS NONETHELESS DISPOSITIVE OF THE ISSUE OF TITLE. FINALLY, DEFENDANTS CONCEDE THAT TENDER NEED NOT BE PLEADED AS AN ELEMENT OF QUIET TITLE IF BASED ON ADEQUATELY-PLEADED VOIDNESS OF THE FORECLOSURE SALE. THE COMPLAINT ASSERTS CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS FOR WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE BASED ON VOID LOAN, THE SUFFICIENCY OF WHICH ARE NOT CHALLENGED BY DEFENDANT MILESTONE AT THE PLEADING STAGE. THEREFORE, DEFENDANT MILESTONE HAVING CONCEDED THAT THE COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS THE VOIDNESS OF THE LOAN, PLAINTIFF IS NOT REQUIRED TO ALLEGE TENDER OF THE AMOUNT DUE ON THE VOID LOAN AS AN ELEMENT OF HER CAUSE OF ACTION FOR QUIET TITLE AGAINST DEFENDANT MILESTONE. DEFENDANTS' ADDITIONAL GROUNDS FOR DEMURRER WHICH WERE RAISED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN REPLY, AND NOT IN RESPONSE TO ARGUMENTS MADE IN THE OPPOSITION, ARE IMPROPER AND WERE NOT CONSIDERED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And To Recover Attorneys' Fees In The Amount Of $10,420
Off Calendar Mar-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Sil Vossler, Esq. representing Plaintiff Karen Moy via Zoom Video Webinar.
Taherian Saman, Esq. representing Defendants Milestone Financial, LLC and William Stuart via Zoom Video Webinar.
Hariss Cohen, Esq. representing Defendants Milestone Financial, LLC and William Stuart via Zoom Video Webinar.

Hearing held.

In re: DEFENDANT MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC DBA MERS HOLDING LTD Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And To Recover Attorneys' Fees In The Amount Of $10,420.

Defense Counsel request to file a supplemental memorandum points and authority and requests the court for a continuance of the motion.
Plaintiff's counsel argues as to the request, does not contest the tentative and states that the lis pendes is proper.
Defense Counsel request to withdraw the motion if the court is not inclined for defense to cure the issue.
The Court denies Defendant's request to withdraw as untimely.

After hearing the arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC DBA MERS HOLDING LTD Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And To Recover Attorneys' Fees In The Amount Of $10,420

is DENIED. Defendant's supporting memorandum is stricken as improper for failure to comply with CRC 3.1113(f). In the absence of a proper supporting memorandum, the motion is denied. (CRC 3.1113(a).) The Court also notes that although Defendant's improper memorandum appears to discuss CCP 405.31, 405.32, and 405.33, as well as CCP 405.38, Defendant's Notice of Motion indicates that the only substantive grounds upon which the motion is made is CCP 405.33.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

DEFENDANT MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC DBA MERS HOLDING LTD, WILLIAM STUART MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Sil Vossler, Esq. representing Plaintiff Karen Moy via Zoom Video Webinar.
Taherian Saman, Esq. representing Defendants Milestone Financial, LLC and William Stuart via Zoom Video Webinar.
Hariss Cohen, Esq. representing Defendants Milestone Financial, LLC and William Stuart via Zoom Video Webinar.

Parties do not contest and submit to the tentative ruling.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC DBA MERS HOLDING LTD, WILLIAM STUART DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

is OVERRULED. The Complaint sufficiently states causes of action for wrongful foreclosure against Defendant William Stuart, and for quiet title against Defendant Milestone Financial LLC. The Complaint sufficiently alleges that Defendant Stuart personally participated in or ratified Defendant Milestone's conduct. The Complaint sufficiently alleges Plaintiff's title and the basis for her title; Defendants cite no authority for the implicit proposition that an instrument which the challenged pleading expressly alleges to be void is nonetheless dispositive of the issue of title. Finally, Defendants concede that tender need not be pleaded as an element of quiet title if based on adequately-pleaded voidness of the foreclosure sale. The Complaint asserts causes of action against Defendants for wrongful foreclosure based on void loan, the sufficiency of which are not challenged by Defendant Milestone at the pleading stage. Therefore, Defendant Milestone having conceded that the Complaint adequately pleads the voidness of the loan, Plaintiff is not required to allege tender of the amount due on the void loan as an element of her cause of action for quiet title against Defendant Milestone. Defendants' additional grounds for demurrer which were raised for the first time in reply, and not in response to arguments made in the opposition, are improper and were not considered.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
